title: Germany's EnBW Signs Deal with Acwa for Ammonia Hydrogen Pathway
content: Trafigura-Backed Miombo Restoration Alliance will invest at least 1 Billion Dollars over 40 years in 4 Large-Scale Carbon Removal Projects across African Miombo Woodlands, Restoring 675,000 Hectares and Targeting over 50 million tons of Greenhouse Gas Removals. The Projects in Malawi, Mozambique, Tanzania and Zambia Combine Woodland Restoration and Agroforestry, share 10â€“60% of Carbon Credit Revenue with Governments and Local Communities, and Aim to Generate High-Quality Carbon Credits that could be worth 2.5 billion Dollars or more if Priced above 50 Dollars per ton.
author: Analysts Account
